```yaml
      how it changes the decision path, and keep the next action narrow and realistic.

      realistic.?'
```

```yaml
      the highest-risk areas clearly, acknowledge the complication explicitly, explain

      production data) and define focused review tasks.

      the highest-risk areas clearly, acknowledge the complication explicitly, explain
```

metadata.probe_axis_text: state the current review posture first, then explain what evidence is still needed to complete a strong review. Keep the ordering easy to follow.

```yaml
internal_capsule:
  mode:
    goal: Apply the Code Review Coordination Skill to the task branch and produce
      an owner-handoff that states the current review posture first, then enumerates
      the evidence still needed to complete a strong review.
    state: "Current review posture: Preliminary/triage stage \u2014 the change has\
      \ been noted but a full, actionable review cannot be completed because core\
      \ review inputs are missing or incomplete. The review is currently focused on\
      \ identifying required context rather than issuing file-level judgments.\nEvidence\
      \ still needed (in priority order):\n  1) Git range: base SHA and head SHA or\
      \ PR link to define the exact diff to review.\n  2) Concise implementation summary:\
      \ one-paragraph description of what changed and why, including the requirement\
      \ or acceptance criteria being satisfied.\n  3) Affected files/modules and a\
      \ short note on the highest-risk areas (stateful logic, auth, data migrations,\
      \ infra).\n  4) Test evidence: unit/integration test names covering the change,\
      \ test results, coverage delta, and any flaky test notes.\n  5) CI status and\
      \ relevant logs for failing or unstable jobs.\n  6) Performance traces or benchmarks\
      \ if reviewers flagged performance concerns.\n  7) Deployment/runbook changes,\
      \ feature flags, and rollback plan for production.\n  8) Any security/infra\
      \ approvals or policy constraints that apply.\nResume instructions: Present\
      \ the posture first, then supply the minimal missing items above so the next\
      \ reviewer can perform a correctness and production-readiness assessment without\
      \ rediscovery."
  route:
  - stage: TRIAGE
    outcome: Map the request to the owner handoff verification branch and verify minimal
      context presence (git range + implementation summary).
  - stage: PLAN
    outcome: Produce a prioritized evidence checklist and target the highest-risk
      files for focused review once context is provided.
  checkpoint:
    owner_transfer: true
    next_action_question: 'What is the minimum confirmed next step needed to advance
      this axis: state the current review posture first, then explain what evidence
      is still needed to complete a strong review. Keep the ordering easy to follow.?'
    stop_condition: Escalate or stop when the required evidence, approval, or policy
      precondition is still missing.
  decision:
    axis: state the current review posture first, then explain what evidence is still
      needed to complete a strong review. Keep the ordering easy to follow.
    judgment: trace_anchor
  slot:
    carrier: true
    visibility: false
```
